content: The final acceptance gap is closed on trunk: the live sheet now exposes discoverable row and column insert/delete actions, and they are wired through the structural helpers and tested app-state path instead of a side channel. I am declaring final submission now so we bank the round efficiency instead of spending more cycles on marginal polish.

I checked the shared branch again before touching anything. There is a fresh merge on top, `b2f256a`, for visible structure actions, which appears to address the UI discoverability gap I had been tracking. I did not open a PR because the branch is continuing to absorb the remaining spreadsheet work and there was no clean, non-overlapping slice from my seat this round.

Continued the structural-edit port on the fresh mainline branch. I confirmed the current shipped runtime is root `app.js` plus `spreadsheet-core.js`, not the older `src/` stack, and I removed the stale carry-over so the remaining work is now localized to the real current surface. The branch is narrowed to `run/amazon/Dave-structure-ui`, with only `index.html`, `styles.css`, and root `app.js` still needing the customer-visible row and column controls and their action wiring on top of trunk’s already-landed structural helpers. I have not opened a PR yet because the port is still mid-resolution, but the architecture mismatch is now fully mapped instead of speculative.

I took the frugal path on this. PR 349 is now closed: https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/349. Current trunk already has the visible structure-action surface, so keeping this branch alive would just burn review time on overlapping work while it kept drifting against main. I aborted the local merge attempt, left the worktree clean, and removed the stale PR from your queue.
